infobox.key: Order of the Crown of Italy
infobox.value: ([Italian](/wiki/Italian_language "Italian language"): **Ordine della Corona d'Italia** or **OCI**) was founded as a national order in 1868 by King [Vittorio Emanuele II](/wiki/Victor_Emmanuel_II_of_Italy "Victor Emmanuel II of Italy"), to commemorate [the unification of Italy](/wiki/Italian_unification "Italian unification") in 1861.[[1]](#cite_note-1) It was awarded in five degrees for civilian and military merit. Today the Order of the Crown has been replaced by the **Order of Merit of Savoy** and is still conferred on new knights by the current disputed head of the house, [Emanuele Filiberto, Prince of Venice](/wiki/Emanuele_Filiberto,_Prince_of_Venice "Emanuele Filiberto, Prince of Venice").

sections: Order of the Crown of Italy
first_paragraph: The **Order of the Crown of Italy** ([Italian](/wiki/Italian_language "Italian language"): **Ordine della Corona d'Italia** or **OCI**) was founded as a national order in 1868 by King [Vittorio Emanuele II](/wiki/Victor_Emmanuel_II_of_Italy "Victor Emmanuel II of Italy"), to commemorate [the unification of Italy](/wiki/Italian_unification "Italian unification") in 1861.[[1]](#cite_note-1) It was awarded in five degrees for civilian and military merit. Today the Order of the Crown has been replaced by the **Order of Merit of Savoy** and is still conferred on new knights by the current disputed head of the house, [Emanuele Filiberto, Prince of Venice](/wiki/Emanuele_Filiberto,_Prince_of_Venice "Emanuele Filiberto, Prince of Venice").

The **Order of the Crown of Italy** ([Italian](/wiki/Italian_language "Italian language"): **Ordine della Corona d'Italia** or **OCI**) was founded as a national order in 1868 by King [Vittorio Emanuele II](/wiki/Victor_Emmanuel_II_of_Italy "Victor Emmanuel II of Italy"), to commemorate [the unification of Italy](/wiki/Italian_unification "Italian unification") in 1861.[[1]](#cite_note-1) It was awarded in five degrees for civilian and military merit. Today the Order of the Crown has been replaced by the **Order of Merit of Savoy** and is still conferred on new knights by the current disputed head of the house, [Emanuele Filiberto, Prince of Venice](/wiki/Emanuele_Filiberto,_Prince_of_Venice "Emanuele Filiberto, Prince of Venice").

The order has been suppressed by law since the [foundation of the Republic](/wiki/Birth_of_the_Italian_Republic "Birth of the Italian Republic") in 1946. However, [Umberto II](/wiki/Umberto_II_of_Italy "Umberto II of Italy") did not abdicate his position as *[fons honorum](/wiki/Fons_honorum "Fons honorum")* and it remained under his Grand Mastership as a dynastic order. While the continued use of those decorations conferred prior to 1951 is permitted in Italy, the crowns on the ribbons issued before 1946 must be substituted for as many five pointed stars on military uniforms.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

## Grades

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Order_of_the_Crown_of_Italy&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Grades")]

The various degrees of the order, with corresponding ribbons, were as follows:

| Ribbon | Class (English) | Class (Italian) | Manner of wear |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  | Knight Grand Cross | *Cavaliere di Gran Croce decorati del Grande Cordone* | Badge on sash on right shoulder, plus star on left chest |
|  | Grand Officer | *Grande Ufficiale dell'Ordine della Corona d'Italia* | Star on left chest |
|  | Commander | *Commendatore dell'Ordine della Corona d'Italia* | Badge on necklet |
|  | Officer | *Ufficiale dell'Ordine della Corona d'Italia* | Badge on ribbon with rosette on left chest |
|  | Knight | *Cavaliere dell'Ordine della Corona d'Italia* | Badge on ribbon on left chest |

## Members

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Order_of_the_Crown_of_Italy&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Members")]

Members of the order have included:

* [Gregor Carl Georg Aminoff](/wiki/Aminoff_family "Aminoff family") (1872-1934), Swedish chamberlain
* [Isaac Artom](/wiki/Isaac_Artom "Isaac Artom") (1829–1900), Italian writer diplomat, and politician
* [Harry Woodburn Blaylock](/wiki/Harry_Woodburn_Blaylock "Harry Woodburn Blaylock") (1878–1928), Canadian lawyer and businessman[[3]](#cite_note-dcb-3)
* [Aaron Bradshaw Jr.](/wiki/Aaron_Bradshaw_Jr. "Aaron Bradshaw Jr.") (1894–1976), [United States Army](/wiki/United_States_Army "United States Army"); [World War II](/wiki/World_War_II "World War II"), commanded [anti-aircraft](/wiki/Anti-aircraft_warfare "Anti-aircraft warfare") forces of [U.S. Fifth Army](/wiki/United_States_Army_North "United States Army North")
* [John Buchan](/wiki/John_Buchan "John Buchan") (1875–1940), Scottish novelist and diplomat
* [Esteban Campodónico](/wiki/Esteban_Campod%C3%B3nico "Esteban Campodónico") (1866-1938), Italian-Peruvian medical doctor, university professor, and philanthropist
* [Vice Admiral](/wiki/Vice_Admiral "Vice Admiral") [Felice Napoleone Canevaro](/wiki/Felice_Napoleone_Canevaro "Felice Napoleone Canevaro") (1838–1926), Italian admiral and diplomat
* Sydney Wentworth Carroll (1877-1958), theatrical producer, after production of *Napoleon: The Hundred Days*, written by [Benito Mussolini](/wiki/Benito_Mussolini "Benito Mussolini")[[4]](#cite_note-4)[[5]](#cite_note-5)[[6]](#cite_note-6)
* [Major-General](/wiki/Major-general_(United_Kingdom) "Major-general (United Kingdom)") [Walter Clutterbuck](/wiki/Walter_Clutterbuck "Walter Clutterbuck") (1894–1987), [British Army](/wiki/British_Army "British Army"), [World War II](/wiki/World_War_II "World War II")
* [Admiral](/wiki/Admiral "Admiral") [Angelo Ugo Conz](/w/index.php?title=Angelo_Ugo_Conz&action=edit&redlink=1 "Angelo Ugo Conz (page does not exist)") [[it](https://it.wikipedia.org/wiki/Angelo_Ugo_Conz "it:Angelo Ugo Conz")] (1871-1948), [Italian Navy](/wiki/Italian_Navy "Italian Navy"), [World War I](/wiki/World_War_I "World War I"), Italian Admiral and Senator
* [Adolf von Deines](/wiki/Adolf_von_Deines "Adolf von Deines") (1845–1911), Prussian diplomat and [General of the Cavalry](/wiki/General_of_the_Cavalry_(Germany) "General of the Cavalry (Germany)")
* [Arthur Conan Doyle](/wiki/Arthur_Conan_Doyle "Arthur Conan Doyle") (1859–1930), Scottish statesman and [Sherlock Holmes](/wiki/Sherlock_Holmes "Sherlock Holmes") author
* [Commander](/wiki/Commander_(Royal_Navy) "Commander (Royal Navy)") [Sir Thomas Fisher](/w/index.php?title=Sir_Thomas_Fisher&action=edit&redlink=1 "Sir Thomas Fisher (page does not exist)"), RN, British naval officer, managing director of the Canadian Pacific Steamship Company
* [James Whitelaw Hamilton](/wiki/James_Whitelaw_Hamilton "James Whitelaw Hamilton") (1860–1932), Scottish artist, member of the [Royal Scottish Academy](/wiki/Royal_Scottish_Academy "Royal Scottish Academy")
* [William Ernest Powell Giles](/wiki/William_Ernest_Powell_Giles "William Ernest Powell Giles") (1835–1897), Australian explorer and gambler
* [Thomas Hanbury](/wiki/Thomas_Hanbury "Thomas Hanbury") (1832–1907), English philanthropist and creator of the [Giardini Botanici Hanbury](/wiki/Giardini_Botanici_Hanbury "Giardini Botanici Hanbury")
* [Major General James Murray Robert Harrison](/wiki/James_Harrison_(British_Army_officer) "James Harrison (British Army officer)") DSO, CB (1880–1957), [Royal Artillery](/wiki/Royal_Artillery "Royal Artillery"), [British Army](/wiki/British_Army "British Army"), in recognition of services on the [Italian front](/wiki/Italian_front_(World_War_I) "Italian front (World War I)") during [World War I](/wiki/World_War_I "World War I")
* [Inoue Kaoru](/wiki/Inoue_Kaoru "Inoue Kaoru") (1836–1915), Japanese politician and a member of the [Genrō](/wiki/Genr%C5%8D "Genrō")
* [Vice Admiral Jules James](/wiki/Jules_James "Jules James") (1885–1957) Commander, [U.S. Naval Forces, Mediterranean](/wiki/United_States_Sixth_Fleet "United States Sixth Fleet") at the close of World War II, decorated by the last [King of Italy](/wiki/King_of_Italy "King of Italy"), [Umberto II](/wiki/Umberto_II_of_Italy "Umberto II of Italy") during his 34-day reign in 1946
* [Rear Admiral](/wiki/Rear_Admiral "Rear Admiral") [Katō Hiroharu](/wiki/Kat%C5%8D_Kanji "Katō Kanji") (1870–1939), [Imperial Japanese Navy](/wiki/Imperial_Japanese_Navy "Imperial Japanese Navy"); Grand Officer in 1920[[7]](#cite_note-7)

Kerr") (1900–1977), [United States Army](/wiki/United_States_Army "United States Army") general, World War II member of the [Allied](/wiki/Allies_of_World_War_II "Allies of World War II") mission to the Italian [Royal Army](/wiki/Royal_Italian_Army "Royal Italian Army")
* Edward King, Viscount Kingsborough, in recognition of his work in researching and compiling his ['Antiquities of Mexico'](/w/index.php?title=%27Antiquities_of_Mexico%27&action=edit&redlink=1 "'Antiquities of Mexico' (page does not exist)")

Parker") (1868–1929), American lawyer and politician, member of the [War Industries Board](/wiki/War_Industries_Board "War Industries Board") and arbiter with [Germany](/wiki/Germany "Germany"), [Austria](/wiki/Austria "Austria"), and [Hungary](/wiki/Hungary "Hungary") following World War I[[8]](#cite_note-8)

## Order of Merit of Savoy

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Order_of_the_Crown_of_Italy&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Order of Merit of Savoy")]

The Order of Merit of Savoy was founded by [Vittorio Emanuele, Prince of Naples](/wiki/Vittorio_Emanuele,_Prince_of_Naples "Vittorio Emanuele, Prince of Naples"), on 23 January 1988, within the framework of the [Civil Order of Savoy](/wiki/Civil_Order_of_Savoy "Civil Order of Savoy")“. Current Grand Master is [Emanuele Filiberto, Prince of Venice](/wiki/Emanuele_Filiberto,_Prince_of_Venice "Emanuele Filiberto, Prince of Venice").[[11]](#cite_note-11)

The insignia are the same as those of the Civil Order, but with the white [vitreous enamel](/wiki/Vitreous_enamel "Vitreous enamel") of the Order of the Crown. The ribbon is blue with a broad white centre stripe.

The order has the same classes as the Order of the Crown, additionally a Gold Cross of Merit and a Silver Cross of Merit is awarded below the Knight's cross.

### Recipients (amongst others)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Order_of_the_Crown_of_Italy&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Recipients (amongst others)")]

As of the year 2000 there are/were 1453 recipients of the Order of Merit.[[12]](#cite_note-12)

* [Clotilde Courau](/wiki/Clotilde_Courau "Clotilde Courau"), Princess of Venice
